methodsAttempted.query: site:kmutt.ac.th KMUTT generative AI policy OR guideline OR academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official KMUTT research-integrity workshop pages, but those were event/training announcements rather than direct policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:kmutt.ac.th ปัญญาประดิษฐ์ มจธ นโยบาย OR แนวปฏิบัติ
methodsAttempted.notes: Thai-language search found the official KMUTT announcement on principles and guidelines for using Artificial Intelligence Tools for content creation.
Fetched robots.txt and the official WordPress announcement wrapper with the OpenClaw crawler user agent. Robots.txt allows /wp-content/uploads/ and lists the sitemap.

The official announcement wrapper embeds and links the PDF AItools24_Announcement.pdf. Static PDF text extraction returned no embedded text, so the PDF was rendered locally for visual verification only.

artifacts.summary: Official KMUTT sources include a March 2024 university announcement PDF on principles and guidelines for using Artificial Intelligence Tools for content creation. Training and workshop materials were recorded but rejected as policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: When students, learners, or personnel use AI tools to create content, KMUTT requires them to cite and clearly state the scope of that use; concealing such use is treated as an ethical violation that may be penalized under relevant rules.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-scope claim from an official KMUTT announcement PDF.
Approved because the Thai source directly requires citation and clear disclosure of AI-tool-use scope and describes concealed use as an ethical violation.
Approved as a teaching/research scope claim without expanding it beyond the announcement language.
Approved with should-level wording matching the Thai term ควร.

artifacts.title: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi AI Tools Guidance
artifacts.summary: Official KMUTT evidence supports staged claims that the university issued Thai-language principles and guidelines for using AI tools to create content for teaching, learning, and research. The claims are limited to disclosure, scope-setting, and assessment-adjustment guidance from the March 2024 announcement PDF.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The source PDF is image-based; static text extraction was not usable, so snippets were verified from rendered pages and kept narrow.
Workshop, presentation, and course results were not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
